message:
fix: bound io syscall buffers via tracked-region check

Adds an EFAULT-on-mismatch guard to the userspace read/write paths so
the libc shim refuses I/O when the caller-supplied buffer is null with
positive length, or when the tracked-region accounting in malloc_abi
says the registered allocation is shorter than the requested transfer
length. Continues the "bound X buffers" series the recent commits have
been building out (a10f7840, 01ec384c, …) — those handled socket,
inet, resolver, time, random and stdlib buffers; this commit closes
the matching gap on the core read/write/flush hot path.

frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs:
* New file-private helper tracked_region_fits(ptr, len) that delegates
  to malloc_abi::known_remaining; returns true for untracked
  pointers (no allocation record) and false only when we have a record
  AND it cannot accommodate len bytes.
* vtable_fd_read / vtable_fd_write set the file's error flag, set
  abi errno to EFAULT, and return -1 when buf is null with count > 0
  or tracked_region_fits(buf, count) is false. The check runs before
  the syscall so callers see a clean EFAULT instead of crashing in
  the kernel.
* vtable_fd_flush re-derives the pending-buffered byte count via
  pos_addr - base_addr (avoids a now-unsafe pointer offset_from when
  the region is bogus) and applies the same EFAULT guard before
  flushing. Replaces the earlier base.is_null() / pos <= base check
  with an address-comparison form so the EFAULT path can short-circuit.

frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:
* read() and write() now compute a single repair_enabled boolean
  (heals_enabled OR Repair action) and reject EFAULT before the
  maybe_clamp_io_len path when repairs are NOT permitted. Repairs
  remain in charge of clamping when enabled, so existing healing
  contracts keep their behavior; only the strict path gains the
  buffer bound. Also calls runtime_policy::observe so the policy
  meter still counts the rejected attempt.

Tests:
* io_internal_native_file_test.rs gains
  vtable_read_rejects_tracked_short_buffer (and a write/flush mirror
  added in the same file) plus the supporting tracked_zeroed_bytes /
  free_tracked / clear_errno / errno_value helpers around the
  malloc_abi tracker, so EFAULT regression coverage uses the real
  allocator path rather than ad-hoc fixtures.
* unistd_abi_test.rs gains analogous read/write coverage that
  toggles repair_enabled to cover both the strict-rejection and
  repair-clamp branches.
